description:
## Overview

Receive error events when the device encounters an unexpected condition. Exception events help diagnose system issues.

## Events

| Event | Description |
|---|---|
| `exceptionEVT` | Error details including radio failures, configuration errors, system issues |

## Indication

Exceptions indicate:
- Something went wrong in system operations
- May require operator attention or intervention
- Potential need for automated recovery or reboot

Note: Currently not fully supported on all device types.

post.description:
## Overview

The `exception` event captures details about exception events in the RFID system, including component-specific errors and system anomalies. These events indicate system failures or error conditions that may require intervention.

Use this event to:
- Monitor system exceptions and errors
- Track radio module issues
- Monitor scanner connectivity
- Track system resource issues (memory, storage)
- Diagnose device failures

---

## Event Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Event Type | Exception Event |
| Communication Type | Device to Cloud |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |

---

## Event Fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `eventName` | Event identifier (exception). |
| `timestamp` | ISO 8601 formatted timestamp of when the exception occurred. |
| `eventNumber` | Unique sequence number for the event. |
| `upTime` | Device uptime when exception occurred (format: X days Xhr Ymin). |
| `details.exceptions.radio` | Radio module exception code and message. |
| `details.exceptions.scanner` | Scanner module exception code and message. |
| `details.exceptions.system` | System exception code and message. |

---

## Exception Types

- **Radio Module** - Issues with RFID radio module (not responsive, configuration errors)
- **Scanner Module** - Barcode scanner issues (connection status, scan progress)
- **System Module** - System-level issues (memory, storage, CPU)

---

## Important Notes

- Exception events are currently not supported in all device configurations.
- Exception information helps diagnose and troubleshoot device failures.
- Critical exceptions may trigger automatic device reboot.

---

## Related Events

| Event | Description |
|---|---|
| `alerts` | Alert events for less critical conditions. |
| `heartbeat` | Periodic health signal including system status. |
| `data_event` | Tag reading events. |

eventName.description: The name of the event indicating an exception occurred.

timestamp.description: The ISO 8601 formatted timestamp of when the event occurred.

eventNumber.description: A unique numerical identifier for the event.

upTime.description: The duration for which the system has been running since the last restart.

details.title: exceptions

anyOf.description: A collection of diagnostic, error, or warning events.

radio.description: Represents diagnostic, error, or warning events related to the radio module.

code.description: A numeric code representing a specific radio-related issue or state.

message.description: A human-readable message describing the radio issue or state.

scanner.description: Represents diagnostic, error, or warning events related to the scanner module.

code.description: A numeric code representing a specific scanner-related issue or state.

message.example: scanner is connected and scanning in progress
message.description: A human-readable message describing the scanner issue or state.

system.description: Represents diagnostic, error, or warning events related to the system.

code.description: A numeric code representing a specific system-related issue or state.

message.description: A human-readable message describing the system issue or state.

details.description: A reference to detailed information about the exceptions that occurred, categorized by components such as 'radio', 'scanner', and 'system'.

scanner.message: scanner is connected and scanning in progress
